Mobile phones and market efficiency in d: This domain examines how mobile phones affect economic development and market functioning in developing regions, drawing on the well-known case of the fishing industry in Kerala, India, and its subsequent critique emphasizing the importance of complex social systems. It also covers the domestication of mobile payments by underserved South African microentrepreneurs and broader reflections on the unpredictable social and economic impacts of mobile technology.
Community and small-scale cellular/netwo: This domain focuses on locally owned and operated telecommunications infrastructure, including small-scale, sustainable cellular networks and community networks financed through community currencies. It also addresses technical challenges such as measurement scheduling strategies in low-resource network environments.
Rural electrification via microgrids and: This domain covers the engineering, planning, and evaluation of microgrid and minigrid systems for rural electrification, spanning cost estimation of micro-hydropower in Nepal, rapid feasibility and business model assessments, DC microgrid power-electronic loss modeling, and multicriteria decision analysis for projects in Sub-Saharan Africa. It also incorporates social perspectives, applying social practice theory to user demand and case studies of standalone, minigrid, and grid-connected system design with irrigation loads in Ethiopia.
Electricity provision and the political: This domain explores the political and institutional dimensions of electricity access, including how electoral cycles influence electricity provision and losses in India. It also addresses tools and education for energy access, such as blackout mapping via smartphones (GridWatch), workshops for electricity access educators, and critical debate over whether technology alone can deliver universal energy access.
Satellite/remote-sensing mapping of infr: This domain applies satellite and remote-sensing imagery with deep learning to map infrastructure and socioeconomic conditions, including poverty mapping, urban village identification, road and infrastructure network extraction, and truck and commercial vehicle traffic detection. It also encompasses nighttime light reconstruction, rare energy infrastructure detection, vulnerability mapping of urban disadvantaged populations, and tracking rural development over decades.
AI for power systems modeling and grid r: This domain uses machine learning and optimization to model, simulate, and strengthen power systems, covering optimal power flow learning, operator learning, power flow benchmark datasets, and open-source microgrid simulation for AI research. It further addresses grid resilience through reinforcement learning and graph-based methods for mitigating cascading failures and fault chains, smart meter data analytics, and integrated resilience platforms like GRIP.
Solar energy forecasting and rooftop/ass: This domain focuses on assessing solar resources and assets, including rooftop solar potential estimation, solar panel mapping via oriented object detection, and experimental study of panel soiling effects in Ghana. It also covers near-term and ultra-short-term photovoltaic power forecasting using satellite and cloud-coverage data, alongside community-oriented solar deployment emphasizing maintenance and empowerment in Haiti.
Sensing and data-driven optimization of: This domain leverages sensing and data-driven methods to characterize and optimize local energy demand and services, including granular hourly energy forecasting from monthly invoices, modeling residential and lighting demand in Kenya and East Africa, and causal analysis of cooking fuel access and emissions. It also addresses off-grid system data sharing in Malawi, battery repurposing for solar storage in Pacific Island communities, latrine sensors for sanitation, and community energy usage and storage analysis.
Disaster and climate risk detection for: This domain applies AI and systems approaches to detect and manage disaster and climate risks to infrastructure, including flood-related waste mapping, building damage assessment from satellite imagery, transportation resilience to floods, and landslide and illegal mining detection. It also covers housing stock characterization from drone images for climate resilience, systems architectures for disaster risk reduction, telecommunications-assisted remote monitoring, and shade simulation for heat resilience.
Infrastructure and design in informal se: This domain examines the physical infrastructure and construction practices of informal settlements, including whether high-mast lighting adequately serves pedestrian paths. It also explores the construction process in its spatial, temporal, and cultural dimensions through the case of Mathare, Nairobi.
Rural telecenters and shared-access digi: This domain examines shared-access computing models that bring digital services to underserved rural populations, drawing on systems like KioskNet and assisted distribution platforms such as BlendNet. It also engages with the practical realities of these deployments through case studies of telecenter sustainability failures in the SARI project in India and telecenters fostering inclusion in Bangladesh. Together, the readings weigh both the technical designs and the socioeconomic challenges of making shared digital access viable and inclusive.
Telecom infrastructure and macroeconomic: This domain explores the relationship between telecommunications infrastructure and broader economic growth in developing nations. It focuses on how investment in and access to telecom services contribute to national and regional development outcomes.
Off-grid and mini-grid rural electrifica: This domain addresses decentralized electrification efforts aimed at bringing reliable power to rural communities lacking grid access. Grounded in a rural microgrid field pilot in India, it considers both the technical goal of ensuring dependable electricity supply and the accompanying social upliftment benefits.
Power grid reliability monitoring and an: This domain applies machine learning and natural language processing to understand and improve energy systems, including identifying indicators of energy burden and analyzing power grid outages in Uganda. The readings emphasize data-driven, knowledge-informed policy approaches to grid reliability. A central theme is leveraging analytics to promote an equitable transition of energy systems.
Rural and last-mile network connectivity: This domain focuses on empirically measuring the state of cellular and data connectivity in rural and last-mile areas, particularly through 2G/3G network measurements and assessments of cellular data in India. It connects these measurement efforts to practical applications such as improving emergency preparedness and response in rural areas. The readings collectively characterize connectivity gaps and their real-world implications.
Digital inclusion for marginalized users: This domain investigates how marginalized users, small non-profits, and underserved communities access and benefit from digital technologies, spanning citizen engagement apps in Windhoek and equitable community internet access during the COVID-19 pandemic. It also addresses securing information systems in an unequal world and understanding the connectivity perspectives of small non-profits serving street children in Bangladesh. The readings emphasize bridging the digital divide across diverse and often overlooked populations.
